rxn_class_summary: Catalyst-free electrochemical [3+2] annulation of N-cyclopropylanilines with alkenes (styrenes) via a radical cation chain mechanism
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of N-cyclopropylaniline (1a) to its aminium radical cation (1a+•) initiates a chain; cathode reduces the product radical cation (3a+•) only in initiation; propagation is via SET between 3a+• and 1a

key_intermediates.name: ring-opened distonic radical cation (β-iminium carbon radical)

electron_flow: The anode oxidizes 1a to the aminium radical cation 1a+•, which undergoes cyclopropane ring-opening and addition to styrene 2a to give the cyclized radical cation 3a+•; 3a+• is then reduced by another molecule of 1a (chain propagation), regenerating 1a+• and releasing neutral product 3a, so only sub-stoichiometric current is needed.

probe_reagents.name: divided H-cell (frit-separated anode/cathode)
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a (23% NMR yield) and [3a+H]+ detected only in the anode chamber, none in the cathode chamber — rules out cathodic reduction as the productive reduction step and supports the chain mechanism

probe_reagents.name: online EC/MS (sonic spray ionization)
probe_reagents.observation: Direct detection of 1a+• (m/z 161) upon applying +3.0 V, confirming anodic generation of the aminium radical cation

probe_reagents.name: catalytic current / Faradaic analysis
probe_reagents.observation: Only ~0.34 equiv of electrons consumed relative to 1a (32.4 C vs 96.5 C required for stoichiometric oxidation), consistent with a radical chain rather than stoichiometric electrolysis

notes: Authors explicitly translate a known photoredox (Ru(bpz)3) [3+2] annulation into a catalyst-free electrochemical version; alternative non-chain pathway (anodic oxidation + cathodic reduction of 3a+•) is considered but disfavored by the divided-cell experiment and the sub-stoichiometric charge consumption. Conditions: undivided RVC(+)/Pt(–) cell, LiOTf/MeCN, 1 mA constant current.
